name: Sodium Hyaluronate (Ophthalmic) | সোডিয়াম হায়ালুরোনেট | Indications, Pharmacology, Dosage, Side Effects & other Generic info with Available Brand names in Bangladesh
indications: Sodium Hyaluronate 0.1% sterile ophthalmic preparation is indicated for all forms of dry eye and for post-surgical lubrication. Sodium Hyaluronate 0.2% sterile ophthalmic preparation is indicated for severe chronic dry eye including after surgery. Both strengths are also indicated for dry eye ...
pharmacology: Sodium hyaluronate is a polysaccharide which functions as a tissue lubricant. It is widely used in ophthalmic surgery because it forms a viscoelastic solution in water which makes it a suitable substitute for aqueous and vitreous humour.
dosage_&amp;_administration: Adult: Instill 1 drop 3 times daily. If necessary this can be increased or as directed by a physician.Use in children: There is no known experience with patients under 18 years old.
contraindications: Known hypersensitivity to any of the components.
side_effects: Transient burning sensation, temporarily blurred vision and transient rise in intraocular pressure. Precautions: To avoid contamination, the tip of the container should not touch any surface and should be fully closed between applications.
pregnancy_&amp;_lactation: Pregnancy category B. Reproductive studies have not demonstrated any risk, but no controlled studies in pregnant women are available. Sodium Hyaluronate has a purely physical effect on the eyes without being absorbed and hence can be used during pregnancy and lactation.
storage_conditions: Keep below 30 C temperature, away from light moisture. Keep out of the reach of children.
